documents.title: Impact of Generative Artificial Intelligence on Education at UC San Diego

ai_strategy.summary: UC San Diego has a campus AI access hub, TritonAI, a research-facing AI profile, a Senate working group report on generative AI in education, and unit-level AI guidance. It also has a notable dedicated health AI leadership role through UC San Diego Health's inaugural Chief Health AI Officer.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: University of California, San Diego's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

narrative_summary: University of California, San Diego (San Diego, CA) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. UC San Diego has a campus AI access hub, TritonAI, a research-facing AI profile, a Senate working group report on generative AI in education, and unit-level AI guidance. It also has a notable dedicated health AI leadership role through UC San Diego Health's inaugural Chief Health AI Officer. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are TritonAI and TritonGPT; Senate working group report on GenAI in education; UC San Diego AI research profile; Chief Health AI Officer role; AI guidelines for communicators. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
